title: Self-Supervised Learning
concept: Pretraining is self-supervised: the training labels are simply the next words in the text, eliminating the need for manual labeling.

exercises.prompt: How does an AI model learn from text during the pretraining stage?

options.text: It predicts the next word in the text automatically
It waits for a human to grade every sentence it reads
It memorizes a dictionary page by page

exercises.explain: Pretraining is self-supervised, meaning the AI learns from raw text by predicting the next word, requiring no manual labels.

cards.title: The Homework Helper That Teaches Itself
cards.body: How do AI models read and learn from billions of pages on the internet? If humans had to label all that data by hand, it would take thousands of years! Instead, AI uses a clever trick: it quizzes itself.

cards.title: What is Self-Supervised Learning?
cards.body: Self-Supervised Learning is a smart training method where the AI creates its own labels from the data itself. In text pretraining, the model takes a sentence, hides the next word, and tries to guess it. Since the actual next word is already in the text, the text itself serves as the answer key!

cards.title: The Coloring Book Analogy
cards.body: Imagine trying to learn how to paint. Instead of an art teacher holding your hand and pointing out every color to use, you are given a coloring book where half-finished drawings are already sketched out. You simply fill in the missing pieces based on the context of the lines around them. You teach yourself by completing the incomplete pictures!

cards.title: Learning from Context
cards.body: If an AI reads the sentence "The cat sat on the ___", it hides the word "mat". The AI guesses "chair", compares it to the hidden word "mat", and learns from its mistake. No human had to label "mat" as the correct answer—the book did it automatically!

cards.title: Myth: AI Needs Constant Human Labeling
cards.body: People often think that AI requires armies of humans to sit and tag every single sentence before training can begin. In reality, modern LLMs do most of their early learning completely on their own using raw text, acting as their own teachers.

cards.title: Comparing Learning Methods
cards.body: Let's compare old-school supervised learning with modern self-supervised pretraining. Notice how the second approach doesn't require human annotators!

left.text: Humans manually label thousands of sentences one by one (slow and expensive).

right.text: The AI hides words in raw text and tries to guess them (fast and unlimited).

exercises.prompt: Where do the training labels come from during the pretraining phase of a language model?

options.text: They are manually written by expert computer scientists
They are simply the next words already present in the training text
They are randomly generated by the computer's processor
They are downloaded from a central AI dictionary database

exercises.explain: In self-supervised pretraining, the training label is simply the actual next word that appears naturally in the text, so no manual human labeling is required.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Self-supervised learning makes it possible to train AI on massive amounts of raw internet text without human labeling.
exercises.explain: Yes! Self-supervised learning allows models to train on billions of web pages because the text itself contains the questions and the answers.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete how self-supervised learning works:
exercises.sentence: Self-supervised learning avoids human work by automatically generating its own ___ from the text.
exercises.explain: Self-supervised learning creates its own labels from the data, which means humans do not need to manually tag every single sentence.

exercises.prompt: Which of these tasks is an example of self-supervised learning?

options.text: Hiding the next word in a news article and trying to guess it
A human rating an AI's response from 1 to 5 stars
Using a calculator to solve a multiplication formula

exercises.explain: Predicting the next word from a real book is self-supervised because the data itself provides the answer key. Rating output quality requires human judgment, and calculating math is rule-based.

exercises.prompt: Why is Self-Supervised Learning considered a massive breakthrough for training giant AI models?

options.text: It requires millions of humans to label every single word first
It creates its own labels from the text by hiding and predicting words
It only works when using supervised images instead of text
It requires the AI to write its own code from scratch

exercises.explain: Self-supervised learning allows models to learn from raw, unlabeled text by predicting missing parts (like the next word). This eliminates the expensive and slow bottleneck of human labeling, letting us train giant models on the entire internet!
